name: Watermelon Ice
recipeInstructions:
1. In a microwave-safe cup, soften gelatin in 2 tablespoons water by heating briefly. Stir until gelatin is dissolved.
2. (Suggestion: Cut up melon in a sieve to catch the seeds, over a bowl to catch the juice; this prevents excess juice around the cutting board.)
3. In a blender container, combine 1 cup of melon cubes, lime (or lemon) juice, honey, and gelatin mixture. Cover and blend at high speed for 30 seconds or until smooth.
4. Add remaining melon in batches, cover, and blend at high speed until smooth. Pour into an 8x8x2-inch pan.
5. Freeze until almost firm.
6. Remove from freezer and transfer the mixture to a large chilled bowl. Beat at high speed with an electric mixer or food processor until smooth.
7. Return to pan or pour into serving size cups or plastic ice cube trays, and freeze several hours until firm.
8. To serve, let stand 15-20 minutes at room temperature. Scrape surface (if using an 8x8 pan) and spoon into serving dishes.
Yield: 6 servings, about 6 cups (I only got about 4 cups)
One serving: Calories 58, Protein 1 g, Fat <1g, Carbohydrate 14 g, Fiber .5g, Cholesterol 0, Sodium 4 mg, Potassium 133 mg.
Diabetic Exchange: 1 fruit
Source: Univ. of Calif. San Diego UCSD Healthy Diet for Diabetes, c. 1990
